src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:516 [High] Interpolating validated-but-unescaped `cwd` into the shell command permits command substitution/metacharacters in valid POSIX directory names, bypassing policy checks that scan only `command`; retain `spawn(..., { cwd })` or use shell-specific escaping.
src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/session/store.mjs:578 [High] Malformed session JSON is now fabricated as a valid empty session; `resumeSession` can subsequently save it over the original file, silently destroying recoverable session data instead of rejecting/quarantining corruption.
src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:727 [High] The exit-file write is not awaited before publishing `donePath`, so `refreshShellJob` can observe completion first, read a missing/partial exit file, and permanently mark a successful auto-backgrounded task failed.
src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/session/manager.mjs:2211 [Medium] The cleanup guard is inverted: cleanup never runs while the server is idle and runs only during active work, allowing stale sessions, tombstones, and runtime state to accumulate indefinitely on idle servers.
src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:465 [Medium] Normal settlement sets `settled = true` before calling `_clearProgressTimer`, making this condition skip `clearInterval`; every completed foreground command with progress enabled leaks a recurring timer.
src/runtime/shared/atomic-file.mjs:38 [Medium] The shortened loop sleeps after the final failed attempt but never performs the corresponding retry; a single custom backoff now provides zero retries, increasing atomic-write/session-save failures under transient rename contention.
Verdict: Reject; the changes introduce security, data-loss, task-status, cleanup, and resource-leak regressions.

[P1] `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:516` — Interpolating `cwd` into shell source permits command substitution/quote injection from crafted directory names and introduces shell-specific `cd` failures; pass `cwd` directly to `spawn`.

[P1] `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/session/manager.mjs:2211` — Inverted guard disables cleanup whenever idle, leaking stale sessions/tombstones, while running sweeps during active work where stale runtime entries may be aborted; restore the active-work early return.

[P1] `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/session/store.mjs:578` — Treating malformed session JSON as a valid empty session lets resume/update paths overwrite the corrupted file, destroying recoverable state; return `null` or quarantine the file.

[P2] `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:727` — The exit-code write is not awaited before the done marker, so observers can see completion first and permanently record a successful job as failed with `exitCode=null`.

[P2] `src/runtime/agent/orchestrator/tools/shell-command.mjs:465` — `settle()` sets `settled=true` before calling this helper, making interval cleanup impossible and leaking one progress timer/closure per completed foreground command.

[P2] `src/runtime/shared/atomic-file.mjs:38` — The shortened loop sleeps after the final failed rename but never performs the post-backoff retry, causing avoidable atomic-write failures under transient Windows locks.

Verdict: Not safe to merge until these correctness, security, and resource-management regressions are fixed.
